mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and pushes S&P 500 higher over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of RSAFS level vs forward 20-day returns stays below 0.02 over any trailing 3-year window, or positive returns are not systematically higher following months with RSAFS above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate consistently below 4% signals an overheated labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: More than 50% of instances with UNRATE below 4% produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or the average return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls while payrolls continue rising, it indicates declining output per worker and potential profit margin erosion, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month while PAYEMS increases over the same month
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or the average return difference between joint-signal months and baseline months is less than -1%.

mechanism_narrative: A real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) above 1% indicates restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions, depressing equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of months with real rate above 1%, or the average return is not significantly negative at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, providing a positive short-term catalyst for S&P 500 returns over a 5-trading-day window.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between HOUST monthly change and forward 5-day SPX returns is less than 0 or not statistically significant (p>0.10) over any rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll gains exceeding 200,000 indicate a robust labor market that supports consumption and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: More than 50% of months following a payroll gain >200k produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or the average return in those months is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing output and economic vitality, which supports S&P 500 earnings expectations and drives positive long-horizon returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO level and next-60-day SPX returns is zero or negative over any 5-year walk-forward window, or the average return after INDPRO above its 6-month moving average is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed holds the funds rate above 4.5% while the unemployment rate is below 4%, the economy is in a late-cycle tightening phase where restrictive policy combined with labor tightness depresses equity risk appetite and forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: More than 40% of months satisfying both conditions produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or the average return under the joint condition is not significantly negative at the 5% level.
